Title: An alternative pathway to plant cold tolerance in the absence of vacuolar invertase activity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes sucrose into glucose and fructose in the vacuole
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with alternative osmoprotectant route by consuming sucrose; loss of activity increases sucrose available for myo-inositol/RFO-associated cold response
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for cold-induced sweetening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout reduces cold-induced sweetening, lowers hexose accumulation and ROS, and increases sucrose and myo-inositol-associated cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in potato cultivars; genotyping of edited alleles; qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; sugar profiling; frying color assay; ROS imaging; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmoprotection and enhanced cold tolerance with reduced oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tuber parenchyma under the apical bud
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in tubers during cold storage; lower in knockout lines after 7 days at 4°C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in tubers during cold storage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold storage at 4°C; whole-plant cold stress at 2°C without irrigation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of VInv in potato reduced vacuolar invertase activity and strongly suppressed cold-induced sweetening. 2. Mutant tubers accumulated more sucrose and less glucose/fructose during cold storage, with improved frying color. 3. Loss of VInv decreased lipid peroxidation and H2O2 accumulation and was associated with induction of osmoprotective myo-inositol/RFO-pathway genes under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Partial knockout of alleles was sufficient to improve CIS resistance; paper proposes that sucrose accumulation in the absence of VInv activity triggers alternative osmoprotective and antioxidant responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for CRISPR editing to reduce CIS and improve postharvest cold tolerance in elite potato cultivars, preferably through transgene-free editing.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious effect on plant growth, green biomass phenotype, or tuber formation was observed under greenhouse conditions in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (33 single-end libraries; no accession reported in text)
